title: Intel(R) Platform Ownership Endorsement Generator - extract command Output
description: JSON output produced by the Intel(R) Platform Ownership Endorsement Generator (poe-gen-tool) 'extract' command. The extract command reads a platform manifest, PCK certificate, or quote file and outputs platform identity information needed for the Platform Ownership Endorsement (POE) provisioning flow.

syntaxVersion.description: Schema version of this JSON output format. This version is incremented only for breaking changes (e.g., removing or renaming fields, changing a field's type or semantics). Additive changes such as new optional fields do not require a version bump — consumers must ignore fields they do not recognize.

platformInstanceId.description: The Platform Instance ID (PIID) that uniquely identifies an Intel SGX or Intel TDX platform. This is a 16-byte value encoded as a lowercase hexadecimal string (32 characters). The PIID is extracted from the SGX Extensions of a PCK Certificate (OID 1.2.840.113741.1.13.1.6) or from a Platform Manifest.

deviceIds.description: An array of Processor Registration IDs (PRIDs), also known as Device IDs. Each PRID uniquely identifies a processor package on the platform. This field is only present when extracting from a Platform Manifest. The number of entries in the array corresponds to the number of processor packages reported in the manifest. In practice, current platforms commonly report up to 8 processor packages; the schema allows up to 1024 entries for future platforms and forward compatibility.

items.description: A single Processor Registration ID (PRID). This is a 16-byte value encoded as a hexadecimal string (32 characters).

oneOf.title: Platform Manifest Extraction
oneOf.description: Information was extracted from a Platform Manifest. Result contains the Platform Instance ID (PIID) and one or more Device IDs (PRIDs).

oneOf.title: PCK Certificate or Quote Extraction
oneOf.description: Information was extracted from a PCK Certificate or an SGX/TD Quote file. Result contains only the Platform Instance ID (PIID).
